profile.name: Bangya Liu
profile.title: Member of Technical Staff, Abaka.ai | Research Scientist, 2077AI Open Source Foundation | Ph.D. Candidate, ECE @ UW–Madison
bio: I work where 3D vision, video generation, and embodied AI feed into each other — getting models to see the geometry of a scene, imagine new versions of it, and act inside it through hands, robots, and vision-language-action policies.

I'm finishing up my Ph.D. at the [MadNets](https://madnets.cs.wisc.edu/) Lab (originally WiNGS) at UW–Madison, advised by [Prof. Suman Banerjee](https://pages.cs.wisc.edu/~suman/), where I bounce between video DiTs, Gaussian splats, and the network plumbing underneath.
Always happy to chat — drop a note about anything.

experience.org: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

projects.title: OmniHaMeR: Foundation Model for Metric Hand Pose Estimation
projects.description: Designing and training a vision foundation model that ingests arbitrary combinations of inputs (RGB, depth, intrinsics, extrinsics, hand masks, wrist views, …) and regresses metrically accurate hand meshes in world coordinates. Unlike prior RGB-only weak-perspective methods that output only root-aligned poses, this work targets absolute MPJPE to support VLA co-training. Adopts a MapAnything-style end-to-end architecture with a MANO head, trained at scale on multiple datasets.

projects.title: CyberArena: Adversarial Cybersecurity Arena for LLM Coding Agents
projects.description: Building a live-combat evaluation environment where attacker and defender agents fight over a shared simulated sandbox under partial observability, moving beyond static CTF benchmarks. Proposes the Defend/Attack Token Ratio (DATR), the first token-level metric quantifying offense/defense economic asymmetry. Empirically tests the “cybersecurity as proof-of-work” thesis across model capability tiers.

projects.title: ByteLOOM: Weaving Geometry-Consistent Human-Object Interactions Videos through Progressive Curriculum Learning
projects.description: Built a human–object interaction video generation model on top of Wan2.1 with realistic human dynamics and diverse object viewpoint synthesis. Curated large-scale open-source hand-object interaction datasets to train the DiT, and introduced a geometry-aware conditioning mechanism that combines multi-view object reference images with relative coordinate maps for consistent generation across viewpoints.

projects.title: SwinGS: Sliding Window Gaussian Splatting for Volumetric Video Streaming with Arbitrary Length
projects.description: A streaming-friendly framework for training, delivering, and rendering volumetric video in real time. Combines spacetime Gaussian with Markov-Chain Monte Carlo to adapt the model across 3D scenes and frames, and uses a sliding window to capture per-frame Gaussian snapshots in a continuous training loop — removing the duration limits of prior dynamic 3DGS approaches.

projects.title: Accelerating Transformer-Based Monocular SLAM via Geometric Utility Scoring
projects.description: Proposed LeanGate, a lightweight feed-forward frame-gating network for transformer-based monocular SLAM. Predicts a per-frame geometric utility score before the heavy geometry foundation model stage, bypassing over 90% of redundant frames; reduces tracking FLOPs by >85% and yields a 5× end-to-end throughput speedup while preserving the accuracy of dense baselines.

projects.title: SpatialStack: Layered Geometry-Semantic Fusion for 3D Spatial Reasoning
projects.description: A hierarchical fusion framework that aligns multi-level geometric features with language backbones for 3D spatial reasoning in VLMs. Unlike prior work that fuses only deep-layer features and discards rich hierarchical signals, SpatialStack progressively stacks features across multiple model depths to jointly capture fine-grained local geometry and broader contextual semantics. The resulting VLM-SpatialStack achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple 3D spatial reasoning benchmarks.

projects.title: Egocentric World Model for Photo-Realistic Hand–Object Interaction Synthesis
CETCam: Camera-Controllable Video Generation via Consistent and Extensible Tokenization
VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
Privacy-Aware Sharing of Raw Spatial Sensor Data for Cooperative Perception
Can Test-Time Scaling Improve World Foundation Model?
MachSim: A Speedy and Scalable Flow Simulator for Traffic Distribution Prediction in Datacenter Networks
projects.description: A scalable flow simulator that models traffic distribution given network topology, forwarding rules, and flow inputs. Treats flows as a directed acyclic graph and uses local flow merging plus multiprocessing to simulate 500K flows across 100 switches in 15 seconds. Integrated into ByteDance network infrastructure to verify configuration updates before production rollout.

projects.title: MEG: A RISC-V-Based System Emulation Infrastructure for Near-Data Processing
Rocket-Chip FPGA Transplant
projects.description: A Verilog wrapper for the Rocket-Chip project plus a Linux boot flow on the Digilent Nexys4DDR. Gives architects an accessible RISC-V platform to boot Linux and evaluate workloads on an affordable FPGA instead of a pure simulator. 137 stars / 29 forks on GitHub.
